title: pilosella bauhini subsp. macra
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. macra is a subspecies of the Pilosella bauhini plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin irritations, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Pilosella bauhini subsp. macra can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
